creator: nick.rozhkov@gmail.com
text:

On 2 =D0=B8=D1=8E=D0=BD=D1=8F 2013, at 21:33, Marcus von Appen =
<mva@FreeBSD.org> wrote:

>=20
> This looks like a pulseaudio<->openal issue for me, rather than
> blender-relevant.It might be that blender, for unknown reasons =
triggers
> this issue (by e.g. passing in some invalid sound device or whatever),
> but foremost, I would guess that something within the pulseaudio =
backend
> of openal-soft is broken.
>=20
> To narrow this down, I would like you to enable openal logging.
>=20
> C shell:
>=20
> # setenv ALSOFT_LOGLEVEL 4
> # setenv ALSOFT_LOGFILE /home/<yourusername>/openal.log
>=20
> Bourne shell:
>=20
> # export ALSOFT_LOGLEVEL=3D4
> # export ALSOFT_LOGFILE=3D/home/<yourusername>/openal.log
>=20
> This will enable some heaving logging for OpenAL and write everything
> into openal.log.
>=20
> Export the both environment variables on a terminal and start blender
> afterwards. This hopefully will provide us with some more information
> about what channel openal-soft passes to pulseaudio. If this also does
> not help us, the next step would be to recompile openal-soft and
> pulseaudio with additional debugging symbols (CFLAGS=3D-g) in order to
> track down the issue within GDB.
>=20
> Thanks for your patience.
>=20
> Cheers
> Marcus
